title: First time run `podman run hello-world` in a vm got stuck
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Hi exprts, I am currently working on a project which package podman binary in a container and then running this container in a vm. The goal of this project is to make users can go inside the container to play with podman.
The issue is not really a bug I think, just a little bit confused. 
Detail is:
    When I shell into the container (host shell => VM => container's sh on host) and pull && run hello-world, the `podman run hello-world` won't return back to shell, it got stuck. And through top cmd I can see that hello-world process is a zombie while conmon process still existed. 
    To break this stuck situation, ctrl-c is enough. And run hello-world again(podman run hello-world) won't got stuck. So this stuck issue only exists when first time running `podman run hello-world`
     I did a little debug, find this [https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/90c2cc6c8342e96ca0a21e59c3cdb7761b2229ea/libpod/oci_attach_linux.go#L222 ](url)
 Because the conn.Read() will never read EOF thus hello-world will got stuck. But why only first time? 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman pull hello-world 

2. podman run hello-world (stuck)

3. ctrl-c

4. podman run hello-world (won't stuck)

**Describe the results you received:**
First time hello-world outputs to the container sh while didn't return back.

**Describe the results you expected:**
container shell is returned 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.0
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.13.8
Git Commit:   d8868746fd3f45b9bfd79e37bf2aa0c7103245fd
Built:        Thu Sep 24 06:01:48 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

In a Virtual Machine, The IO operation from container to VM and then from VM to host is like how kata does.

comments.body: Can you reproduce this error?  IE Is this just the first time you run a rootless container in the VM, and now all future containers work?  If you create a different user inside of the container and run these commands, does it get stuck?
Yes, I can reproduce this error every first time running podman run hello-world through the privileged container's shell. But I haven't try another user, let my try it. Thanks.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I take it that that worked for you with a different user. CLosing,
